mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns. This is an always-on mechanism that operates whenever FEDFUNDS exhibits a downward trend over the preceding 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.16% as of Nov 2017 reflects post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which translates into higher corporate revenue and earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147,241k as of Nov 2017 reflects sustained employment gains
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth triggers do not produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength and construction activity. When both series accelerate simultaneously, this indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate profit growth, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492,717M and HOUST at 1,290k as of Nov 2017
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with housing recovery
failure_condition: Joint trigger conditions do not produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal supply-side weakness or demand destruction. When INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average, equity markets reprrice earnings downward, producing negative 60-day forward returns as the market anticipates profit margin compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: INDPRO below 12-month MA does not produce negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that increases real borrowing costs for corporations and consumers. This dual pressure produces negative 20-day forward returns as equity valuations compress under higher discount rates and lower growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 247.59 and FEDFUNDS at 1.16% as of Nov 2017
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Joint trigger conditions do not produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, equity markets anticipate lower consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of Nov 2017 reflects tight labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp do not produce negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic demand. When GDPC1 accelerates above 2.5% annualized growth, equity markets reprice earnings expectations upward, producing positive 5-day forward returns as growth surprises are incorporated into valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,169.73B (2017 dollars) as of Q3 2017
failure_condition: GDPC1 > 2.5% growth does not produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,200k annualized rate signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand destruction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns as the market reprices growth expectations downward in response to housing sector weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,290k as of Nov 2017 remains above threshold
regime_hint: housing market contraction or demand shock
failure_condition: HOUST < 1,200k does not produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
